name: D-beta-hydroxybutyrate
summary: D-beta-hydroxybutyrate is a ketone-body intervention normalized for C. elegans lifespan and HDAC-linked chromatin evidence.
mechanism_summary: Uses beta-hydroxybutyrate exposure to probe ketone, nutrient-sensing, and HDAC-linked aging mechanisms in preclinical models.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Beta-hydroxybutyrate extended C. elegans lifespan through HDAC-linked biology; D-beta-hydroxybutyrate HDAC C. elegans lifespan study.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
